Returns a list of all supported countries with their configurations. Supports filtering by service type, currency, country code, and active status. Data is aggregated from the Provider Registry for real-time accuracy. The isEnabled flag indicates whether the authenticated integrator has this service enabled and providers attached.

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description: Filter by country code (e.g., KE, NG, GH)

get.description: Returns comprehensive details about a specific country including supported currencies, networks, and service availability. The isEnabled flag indicates whether the authenticated integrator has each service enabled and providers attached.
Returns all active mobile money networks available in a specific country, aggregated from all providers. Indicates if network selection is required.
get.summary: Get available networks for a country

get.description: Returns a list of all supported banks with optional filtering by currency or search term. Banks are sourced from the centralized bank data registry.
Returns all countries that support deposit (onramp) services with their configurations.
Returns all countries that support withdraw (offramp) services with their configurations.
This endpoint is used to check for the health of the application

200.description:
Health check

The Health Check is successful

503.description: The Health Check is not successful

post.description: Checks the phone number and network already on file for the given customer_key against whichever provider would actually handle a payout to them, reducing the risk of sending funds to the wrong recipient.
post.summary: Validate a phone number against a customer name before payout

post.description: This endpoint will create a offramp request for a customer. If the fiat transfer fails after successful crypto receipt, an automatic refund will be initiated after 5 minutes. Use the refund-status endpoint to check refund status.

get.description: This endpoint will return all offramp transactions for the integrator.
This endpoint will return the status of the withdrawal request.
This endpoint will cancel the offramp transaction.
Get the status of an onchain refund. Refunds are automatically initiated 5 minutes after a failed transaction if the crypto was successfully received but fiat transfer failed.

post.description: Retry a failed crypto refund for an offramp transaction. Gas/fund errors can be retried up to 10 times, other errors up to 4 times. Refunds marked for manual intervention cannot be retried through this endpoint.

Retry multiple failed crypto refunds by providing an array of reference IDs. Gas/fund errors can be retried up to 10 times, other errors up to 4 times. Refunds marked for manual intervention will be skipped.

referenceIds.description: Array of offramp transaction reference IDs to retry

post.description: Retry all failed refunds that are eligible for retry. This excludes refunds that are permanently failed (requiresManualIntervention). Contact support if you need to retry refunds marked for manual intervention.

Submit a Lightning bolt11 invoice to receive refund for a failed Lightning offramp. The invoice must be for the exact refund amount, must not be expired, and must not already be paid. This endpoint is only applicable for Lightning (bolt11) offramp transactions where the crypto was received but fiat transfer failed.

invoice.description: Lightning bolt11 invoice for the refund. Must match the exact refund amount.

message.example: Invalid invoice or not eligible for Lightning refund

RATE_SOURCE.description: Which currency the amount field represents. Defaults to 'fiat' (current behavior — fiatAmount holds a fiat value). Set to 'crypto' to instead pass the desired crypto amount in fiatAmount and get back the equivalent fiat cost.

source.description: Which currency the amount field represents. Defaults to 'fiat' (current behavior — fiatAmount holds a fiat value). Set to 'crypto' to instead pass the desired crypto amount in fiatAmount and get back the equivalent fiat cost.

Which currency the amount field represents. Defaults to 'crypto' (current behavior — cryptoAmount holds a crypto value). Set to 'fiat' to instead pass the desired fiat amount in cryptoAmount and get back the equivalent crypto cost.

rate_id.description: Rate ID - use this in crypto bridge deposit request

crypto_amount.description: Crypto amount in smallest unit (e.g., msats for Lightning, wei for ETH)

total_cost.description: Total cost in source currency including all fees

expires_at.description: Rate expiration timestamp (ISO 8601) - 60 seconds from creation

source_amount.description: Source fiat amount customer will pay

deposit_balance.description: Amount of the wallet that has been deposited so far

completed_at.description: Timestamp the transaction reached a terminal status

error_message.description: Error message if the transaction failed

country.description: Country where the wallet is used (optional if derivable from currency) - Cards are accepted globally

idNumber.description: Customer national ID number (required for Capitec Pay in South Africa - 13 digit SA ID)

forceDefaultMethod.description: When true, force the payment method specified in paymentMethod to be the only option presented to the customer (no switching allowed). Passed to the provider as forceDefaultMethod where supported.

bankPaymentDetails.description: Bank payment details for manual bank transfer (e.g. KES PesaLink)

currency.description: Override transaction currency (e.g. USD for USD mobile money in Congo/Zambia). When provided and matches the wallet currency, phone-country currency validation is skipped.

network.description: Mobile network / payout channel (e.g. MPESA, MTN)

transactionError.description: Transaction error message from provider or system

telcoId.description: Telco/MNO transaction ID from the mobile money provider

completed_at.description: When the transaction reached a terminal status

transaction_amount.description: Amount debited from integrator wallet including fees

network.description: Mobile network / payout channel (e.g. MPESA, MTN)

customer_key.description: Customer key associated with the transaction

telco_id.description: Telco/MNO receipt code (e.g. Mpesa confirmation code)

integrator_fee_amount.description: Integrator fee amount charged on the transaction

transaction_error.description: Transaction error message if the transaction failed

address.example: Unit 163 Richmond Street, 25 Allan Road, Oakdene, 4250

phoneNumber.description: Customer Phone Number, Phone number must be in international format

bankCode.description: The code of the bank gotten from the bank list api

bankName.description: Bank name — required for South Africa (e.g. "FNB", "ABSA"). Not needed for Kenya.

accountNumber.description: The account number of the bank

country.description: The country code (ISO-2 like KE, ZA or ISO-3 like KEN, ZAF)

referenceId.description: The reference id of the transaction

callbackUrl.description: Callback URL to receive withdrawal status updates

dob.description: should be of this format YYYY-MM-DD

documentExpirationDate.description: should be of this format YYYY-MM-DD

documentName.description: example PASSPORT, GOVERNMENT ID, VOTERS ID

isActive.description: Is the country active for this service

isEnabled.description: Is this service enabled for the authenticated integrator

requiresNetwork.description: Requires network selection for mobile money

requiresAccountName.description: Requires account name for mobile money

countries.description: List of active countries for this service

savedBeneficiaryId.description: ID of a saved beneficiary on the integrator config — used when beneficiaryDetails is not explicitly provided

overrideUsdRate.description: Admin-supplied exchange rate as "1 USD = X local currency". Overrides the auto-fetched rate for USD snapshot computation. Only meaningful for non-USD wallets.

batchReference.description: Unique reference per batch — idempotency key on retry

callbackUrl.description: Shared callback URL for all batch events

requests.description: One entry per wallet to settle

settlementIds.description: IDs of existing settlements to merge

settlementNotificationEmail.description: Email to receive settlement updates (defaults to profile email)

settlementSlackWebhookUrl.description: Slack incoming webhook URL for settlement notifications

walletId.description: The fiat wallet the payout would be debited from — used to resolve which provider would actually handle it.

customer_key.description: Customer key of an existing mobile-money customer. The phone number, network, and name validated are always the ones already on file for this customer — not arbitrary values — so the result reflects who would actually receive this payout.

usingIntegratedWallet.description: Whether using integrated crypto wallet flow

onchainError.description: Onchain error details if the transaction failed

transactionError.description: Transaction error details if the transaction failed

networkProvider.description: Provider network (Check Payment Providers API for available networks)

phoneNumber.description: Phone number of the bank account holder (optional — not used for the bank transfer itself)

bankCode.description: Bank code (required for Kenya; use 0 for South Africa)

bankName.description: Bank name — required for South Africa (e.g. "FNB", "ABSA", "NEDBANK"). Not needed for Kenya.

country.description: Country code (ISO-2 like KE, GH, NG or ISO-3 like KEN, GHA, NGA)

businessShortcode.description: Till number (Business shortcode) to pay to

paybillNumber.description: Paybill number to send payment to

accountNumber.description: Account number / reference the Paybill uses to identify the sender

bolt11.description: Pre-generated Lightning bolt11 refund invoice. If provided and valid when a refund is triggered, Kotani pays this invoice directly without waiting for you to submit one manually. Lightning invoices expire — provide generate_invoice_url so Kotani can fetch a fresh one if this expires.

payment_hash.description: Payment hash of the bolt11 invoice — used to detect if the invoice was already paid.

amount_msat.description: Amount of the bolt11 invoice in millisatoshis. Must exactly match the crypto amount to be refunded. Kotani validates this before paying.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiry timestamp of the bolt11 invoice. If expires_at is in the past when a refund is triggered, Kotani calls generate_invoice_url for a fresh invoice. Lightning invoices typically expire in 1–24 hours depending on your node settings.

generate_invoice_url.description: URL Kotani calls (POST) to request a fresh bolt11 invoice when the stored one has expired. Kotani sends { amount_msat, reference_id } in the request body and expects { bolt11, payment_hash, amount_msat, expires_at } in the response. If not provided and the invoice expires, Kotani falls back to webhook notification asking you to submit via POST /offramp/submit-refund-invoice/:referenceId.

address.description: Fallback on-chain address for non-Lightning chains (EVM, Solana, Stellar, Cardano etc.). Kotani sends refunded crypto here if the fiat disbursement fails. Must be a valid address for the same chain as the offramp transaction.

lipaNaMpesaReceiver.description: Lipa na M-Pesa (Till/Buy Goods) receiver — Kenya only

currency.description: Fiat currency (e.g. KES, GHS, SLE, TZS)

senderAddress.description: Sender address (optional - if not provided, will use integrator crypto wallet)

rateId.description: Rate ID obtained from the /offramp/rate endpoint

refund_config.description: Optional refund destination for this transaction. For Lightning: provide a bolt11 invoice so Kotani can refund immediately if fiat disbursement fails, without waiting for you to submit one manually. Include generate_invoice_url so Kotani can refresh the invoice if it expires. For on-chain chains: provide address as the fallback refund destination. If omitted, Kotani notifies you via webhook and email when a refund is needed and you submit via POST /offramp/submit-refund-invoice/:referenceId.

referenceId.description: The reference ID of the onramp crypto transaction

status.description: The status of the onramp crypto transaction

cryptoWallet.description: The ID of the crypto wallet

chain.description: The chain of the onramp crypto transaction

token.description: The token of the onramp crypto transaction

amountToSend.description: Total crypto the sender must dispatch (cryptoAmountReceived + feeInCrypto)

transactionHash.description: The transaction hash of the onramp crypto transaction

receiverAddress.description: The receiver address of the onramp crypto transaction

chain.description: The chain of the onramp crypto transaction

token.description: The token of the onramp crypto transaction

referenceId.description: The reference ID of the onramp crypto transaction

callbackUrl.description: Webhook URL to notify when the crypto transfer completes or fails.

providerNetwork.description: Provider network (Check Payment Providers API for available networks)

paymentMethod.description: Payment method should be CARD OR PAYBYBANK

fiatWalletId.description: Optional. Links this transaction to a specific fiat wallet for currency resolution and audit. No fiat is debited — crypto is sent directly from your pre-loaded crypto wallet. If omitted, a wallet is auto-selected by currency.

fiatFee.description: Platform fee charged on the fiat side

fiatAmountToSend.description: Total fiat the customer must pay (fiatAmount + fiatFee)

error.description: Error details if the transaction failed

network.description: Mobile network provider (e.g. MPESA, MTN, AIRTEL)

currencyBreakdown.description: Breakdown by currency - never add amounts from different currencies

fee.description: Total fee charged (provider cost + integrator fee)

transactionAmount.description: Total amount deducted from wallet (amount + fee)

to.description: Phone number to send SMS to

message.example: Hello, this is a test message

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL to receive SMS notifications
